chunk_summary: This section outlines follow-up intervals for children with undernutrition, emphasizing age, risk level, and clinical context, with specific guidance for infants under 6 months, SAM, MAM, and stunting cases, based on Indian and global guidelines.
learning_objectives: Understand the recommended follow-up intervals for different undernutrition classifications, including SAM, MAM, and stunting.
Learn the specific monitoring requirements for infants under 6 months at risk of undernutrition.
Recognize the importance of community linkage and home visits post-discharge from inpatient SAM care.
Apply age-sensitive and risk-based principles to determine follow-up frequency for children with faltering growth.
key_facts: Infants under 6 months at risk (##U6M##) should be monitored weekly, with reduced frequency only after sustained weight gain for two consecutive weeks.
Uncomplicated SAM cases (e.g., MUAC <115 mm or WHZ/WLZ < -3) require weekly follow-ups initially, with appetite reassessment at each visit.
MAM cases typically require fortnightly to monthly follow-ups, with increased intensity during weight plateau, recurrent illness, or food insecurity.
Stunting without wasting should involve length reassessment every 3 months, with more frequent weight checks in younger children or during illness.
Post-discharge from inpatient SAM care, weekly home visits are recommended for the first 4 weeks, then every 2 weeks until program discharge.
reference_quote: After discharge from inpatient SAM care, Indian operational guidance emphasizes intensive community linkage: prioritize weekly home visits in the first four weeks, then every two weeks until program discharge.
